behavior_summary: This script implements the behavior for a 'child' NPC: housekeeping when leaving a small trigger area, simple reactions when looked at or targeted/cast on, and a large multi-branch dialog sequence when used (interacted with).
U8P_CHILD::CHILD::leaveFastArea clears several stored references for the NPC and marks the NPC as excluded from processing (cleanup) when the Avatar leaves the child's fast/trigger area.
U8P_CHILD::CHILD::look responds to being examined by checking death state and producing a short bark ('dead child' or 'child').
U8P_CHILD::CHILD::cast handles being targeted or cast-on: it checks the NPC's target, may spawn guard or helper processes, respects death state, and selects frightened barks via random choice.

U8P_CHILD::CHILD::use is the interactive dialog handler: it constructs response strings, builds choice lists, presents questions with Item::ask, compares player replies (strcmp) to branch through a large conversation tree, spawns follow-up behaviors, and sets NPC activity when finished.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CHILD::CHILD::leaveFastArea: triggered by Event 10 (U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea) -> set info/process-exclude -> call resetRef repeatedly for specific ref IDs -> return immediately (cleanup-only flow).
U8P_CHILD::CHILD::look: set info/exclude -> call Npc::isDead -> choose one of two static barks -> call Item::bark -> suspend/return.

U8P_CHILD::CHILD::cast: check Npc::getTarget; if no target spawn a FREE helper; if target == Avatar (1) possibly spawn guard (CALGUARD) and FREE helper; check death state; use urandom to select between frightened barks; call Item::bark and spawn follow-ups as needed.

U8P_CHILD::CHILD::use: builds strings (getName/concat), registers or frees string resources (FREE::2A56), uses urandom to vary greetings, creates slists and calls Item::ask to get player choices, branches heavily by comparing returned strings with known phrases (strcmp), may spawn other methods (METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090, FREE::1C19), and sets NPC activity via Npc::cSetActivity at ends of dialog branches.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of each resetRef id (0x021C, 0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) — likely references to objects/areas/scripts associated with this child but not canonicalized.
Precise effects of FREE::2A56 (appears to register/clean/free constructed strings or list resources for dialog UI) and whether it performs display-time side effects.
Concrete behaviors of spawned entry points METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090, FREE::1C19 and CALGUARD::0080 (inferred to animate or call guards but exact gameplay effect is unknown).
Whether any global quest flags are toggled by U8P_CHILD::CHILD::use branches (none observed in disassembly, but dialog may logically alter state elsewhere).

main_function.name: U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Housekeeping/cleanup when the child NPC's trigger area is exited: mark/exclude the NPC from processing and clear several stored reference slots associated with the NPC.
entry_conditions: Event 10: U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea — invoked when the Avatar or relevant actor leaves the child's small trigger region

function_map.name: U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Cleanup when leaving fast/trigger area; resets stored references and excludes NPC from processing.

function_map.name: U8P_CHILD::look
function_map.role: Examine/look handler: check death state and produce an appropriate short bark ('dead child' or 'child').

function_map.name: U8P_CHILD::cast
function_map.role: Handler for being targeted/cast-on: may spawn helper/guard processes, checks target and death, and emits frightened barks chosen by randomness.

function_map.name: U8P_CHILD::use
function_map.role: Primary interaction/dialog routine: builds strings, constructs choice lists, asks the player questions (Item::ask), branches heavily based on replies, spawns follow-ups, and sets NPC activity.

external_calls.purpose: Clear/reset stored reference slots for this NPC (called repeatedly with different reference ids: 0x021C, 0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200).
Check whether the NPC is dead to select the correct bark ('dead child' vs 'child') or abort further actions.
Emit a short examination string to the examining process/player.
Determine who is targeting the child (used to decide if the Avatar is the aggressor and whether to spawn guards/helpers).
Create helper/free processes or guard-calling routines in response to being targeted (e.g., spawn FREE helper or CALGUARD).
Select between alternate frightened barks ('Mommy!', 'Ahhh! Bad man!') to vary reaction.
Avoid barking/response if NPC is dead (abort behavior).
Emit frightened bark lines when targeted.
Obtain the NPC's name to construct 'I am <name>.' and other personalized responses.
Assemble response strings for the dialog (e.g., 'I am <name>.').
Register/clean/free or otherwise manage constructed strings/lists used in the dialog system (called after building strings and lists).
Randomize greeting variants and some dialog choices.
Build a slist of choices and present a multiple-choice prompt to the player; receive the player's selected reply to branch dialog.
Compare the player's reply string against known phrases to pick dialog branches.
Start follow-up behavior scripts (METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090, FREE::1C19) or other side-effectful processes as part of dialog outcomes.
Emit lines of dialog during the interaction sequence.
Set the NPC's activity state (e.g., idle) when dialog branches finish.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the behavior for a 'child' NPC: housekeeping when leaving a small trigger area, simple reactions when looked at or targeted/cast on, and a large multi-branch dialog sequence when used (interacted with).
